name: claude-code-quota-bar
description: See which Claude Code conversations are working, click to jump to the right VS Code window, and hear when Claude needs you. Plus 5h/7d/scoped quota bars coloured by projected burn-rate pace, not just %used. Unofficial, Windows-only.

commands.title: Claude Code Quota: Open Usage Page

claudeCodeQuotaBar.refreshIntervalMinute.description: How often to refresh the usage data, in minutes.

claudeCodeQuotaBar.burnRateGreenMax.description: Burn-rate pace (percent used divided by percent of window elapsed) at or below which a quota bar is coloured green — the projected end-of-window usage is comfortably under the quota.

claudeCodeQuotaBar.burnRateYellowMax.description: Burn-rate pace at or below which a quota bar is coloured yellow instead of red. At the default of 1.0, red means the projected end-of-window usage exceeds 100% of the quota.

claudeCodeQuotaBar.sounds.enabled.description: Play a system sound when a conversation finishes replying or hands control back to you (a question, a permission prompt). Off by default — no surprise sound on install. Toggle from the speaker icon at the top of the panel, or here.

claudeCodeQuotaBar.braveUserDataDir.description: Path to a dedicated Brave user-data directory (e.g. C:\OctopusData\BraveOctopus) with a claude.ai session logged in, used for the fast cookie-based usage fetch. Empty by default: the cookie path is disabled cleanly (no browser spawn) and the extension falls back to your Claude Code OAuth token instead. Set BRAVE_EXE if brave.exe isn't in the standard Program Files location.
